This pre-season match is part of the academy's preparations for the new season, allowing players to gain valuable experience against competitive opposition.

articles.content_html:
Rochdale Football Club will celebrate its promotion back to the football league at the Classic Car Show this Sunday. The club secured its place in the league in a dramatic playoff final at Wembley in May.

The event will provide an opportunity for fans and the community to come together and commemorate the team's achievement. Details about the show and its activities have been shared on social media.

articles.title: Rochdale Secures Season-Long Loan for Chelsea Defender Kaiden Wilson
articles.excerpt: Rochdale has completed a season-long loan deal for Chelsea defender Kaiden Wilson, who is expected to make his competitive debut in the upcoming EFL Cup.
articles.content_html:
Rochdale AFC has secured a season-long loan for Chelsea centre-back Kaiden Wilson, as the club continues to strengthen its squad ahead of the new season. The 20-year-old defender joins the Dale after making six appearances for Chelsea’s Under-21s in the EFL Trophy, although he has yet to feature for the senior team.

Wilson, who was born on September 2, 2005, is under contract with Chelsea until June 30, 2030. His arrival at Spotland is seen as a strategic move by Rochdale, who are looking to enhance their defensive options with a player who has a promising background in youth football.

The young defender could make his competitive debut for Rochdale in the EFL Cup preliminary round against Tranmere Rovers on Saturday, August 1. Fans are eager to see how Wilson adapts to first-team football after his development at Chelsea, where he was a key player in their successful Under-21 side last season.

Wilson's loan is part of a broader trend at Chelsea, where the club is offloading several academy players to gain valuable senior experience. This approach is intended to ensure that young talents receive the necessary playing time while maintaining the club's asset value. Chelsea's strategy has led to multiple temporary departures, with other players also expected to be loaned out this summer.

articles.title: Rochdale AFC Signs Goalkeeper Nils Ramming on Loan
articles.excerpt: Rochdale AFC has announced the loan signing of goalkeeper Nils Ramming, enhancing their squad ahead of the upcoming season.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 17 July 2026 at 13:26. 3 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale AFC has announced the loan signing of goalkeeper Nils Ramming, enhancing their squad ahead of the upcoming season.

Latest update

Rochdale have signed Chelsea defender Kaiden Wilson and Brighton goalkeeper Nils Ramming on season-long loan deals.

Kaiden Wilson, 20, is contracted to Chelsea until the end of the 2029-30 season. He has made six appearances for their Under-21s in the EFL Trophy but is yet to make his senior debut for the club.

Nils Ramming, 19, has been capped by Sweden at Under-19 level and joined Brighton from Eintracht Frankfurt last year.

Both players could make their competitive debuts for Rochdale when they face Tranmere in an EFL Cup preliminary round tie on Saturday, 1 August.

Earlier developments

Rochdale AFC has confirmed the loan signing of goalkeeper Nils Ramming from an undisclosed club. The move aims to bolster the team's options in goal as they prepare for the new season.

Ramming is expected to bring valuable experience and skill to the Rochdale squad. The club expressed their excitement about the addition of the young goalkeeper.

The loan deal is part of Rochdale's strategy to strengthen their team ahead of competitive fixtures. Ramming will join the squad for pre-season training and is likely to feature in upcoming matches.

Further details regarding his debut will be announced in due course.

Update timeline

  • 17 July 2026, 13:26: Rochdale Sign Kaiden Wilson and Nils Ramming on Loan
  • 17 July 2026, 13:01: Rochdale AFC Signs Goalkeeper Nils Ramming on Loan

articles.title: Rochdale Hornets Defeated by Wigan in Lancashire Cup
articles.excerpt: In the Lancashire Cup 1946/47, Rochdale Hornets lost to Wigan with a score of 32 to 5 in the second leg of the first round.
articles.content_html:
In the Lancashire Cup 1946/47, Rochdale Hornets faced Wigan in the second leg of the first round.

The match concluded with Rochdale Hornets scoring 5 points, while Wigan secured a decisive victory with 32 points.

This result marks a significant moment in the history of both clubs, reflecting the competitive nature of the tournament during that season.

The match took place on July 17, 2026, and is part of the ongoing documentation of rugby league history.

articles.title: Rochdale AFC Suffers 2-0 Defeat in Pre-Season Friendly Against FC United
articles.excerpt: Rochdale AFC manager Ian Watson reflects on the team's recent outing against FC United, highlighting key moments and future aspirations.
articles.content_html:
ONGOING STORY — Updated 14 July 2026 at 21:52. 7 source updates have been combined into this article.

Rochdale AFC manager Ian Watson reflects on the team's recent outing against FC United, highlighting key moments and future aspirations.

Latest update

Rochdale AFC's first pre-season outing ended in a 2-0 defeat at FC United of Manchester on Tuesday evening. The match marked the beginning of the team's preparations ahead of the upcoming season.

Despite a spirited performance, Rochdale struggled to find the back of the net, while FC United capitalised on their chances to secure the win.

The defeat serves as an early reminder of the challenges ahead as the team looks to build momentum in the weeks leading up to the new season.

Fans will be hoping for improved results in the upcoming fixtures as the squad continues to develop and refine their tactics.

Earlier developments

Rochdale AFC manager Ian Watson has spoken about the team's recent outing against FC United. In his comments, he reflected on the performance and the lessons learned from the match.

Watson noted the importance of maintaining focus and discipline throughout the game. He emphasised that every match provides an opportunity for growth and improvement.

Looking ahead, Watson expressed optimism about the team's potential and the upcoming fixtures. He believes that with continued effort, the squad can achieve its goals this season.

The manager's insights come as Rochdale AFC prepares for its next challenge, aiming to build on the experiences gained from the FC United match.
